title: A Line of GitHub Code Reveals AMD: Anthropic Exposed as Its New Customer, Accelerating the Shift Away from NVIDIA
excerpt: A line of public code that should not have exposed commercial secrets has revealed Anthropic's next chip layout. According to the chip industry analysis firm Se
content_html:
A line of public code that should not have exposed commercial secrets has revealed Anthropic's next chip layout. According to the chip industry analysis firm SemiAnalysis, the code published on GitHub by AMD's senior director of AI business clearly leaves traces of Anthropic as an AMD customer. This non-official, non-media leak sends a clear signal: Anthropic is actively expanding its chip sources, freeing its compute suppliers from a single list.

On Monday, SemiAnalysis posted an analysis on the social platform X, and the conclusion came from a professional dissection of the public code by AMD's senior director. The code content directly involves information related to Anthropic as an AMD customer. The institution stated that it will further explain the code details and background. If this judgment is correct, AMD will gain a new position in the high-end AI training market previously dominated by NVIDIA and Google, and Anthropic's computing infrastructure will thus accelerate its move towards diversification.

The flow path of this information is quite special. It did not come through official announcements or news reports, but rather through code pushed to GitHub by an internal executive of AMD. SemiAnalysis deciphered this code and then publicly shared the conclusions on X. In the technology industry, such cases of accidental leaks through public code repositories are not uncommon—GitHub is inherently searchable, and the publisher was the senior director of AMD's AI business, which combined makes the credibility of the information quite high. In the post, SemiAnalysis promised to continue breaking down the code logic layer by layer, providing the market with a more complete interpretation.

In fact, before this line of AMD was exposed, Anthropic's computing procurement had already been far from a single path. Google's TPU (Tensor Processing Unit) is one of its important computing sources, which is closely related to Google's strategic investment in Anthropic; additionally, Samsung has also been included in its supplier system. The existing layout indicates that Anthropic has never bet on a single point, but has deliberately created a diversified supplier matrix. The addition of AMD will only make this network have another high-performance computing option outside of NVIDIA GPUs.

For Anthropic, bringing AMD into the alliance carries multiple strategic weights. As model training and inference scale continue to expand, the supply chain risks, suppressed bargaining power, and the drawbacks of being locked into a specific technical path brought by a single chip supplier have become increasingly evident. By embracing Google TPU, Samsung, and AMD at the same time, it can re-find a better balance between cost control, supply stability, and technological flexibility. For AMD, winning over Anthropic is a landmark victory—this company has been continuously investing in data center AI chips, and its MI series GPUs are considered one of the main competitors to NVIDIA's H series products; once Anthropic officially joins, AMD will not only enhance its presence among top AI laboratories, but also bring real incremental revenue for its data center AI chip business.

Anthropic's move reflects a systemic shift in the entire industry. As OpenAI, Google DeepMind, Meta AI, and other leading institutions continue to increase their demand for computing power, AI companies are collectively evaluating and expanding their chip supply sources, just to avoid the risk of putting all eggs in one basket. This collective turn objectively provides AMD, Intel, and other companies chasing NVIDIA, an opening to enter the core AI customer supply chain.

sentences.zh: 通过同时拥抱谷歌TPU、三星和AMD，可以在成本控制、供应稳定性和技术灵活性之间重新找到更好的平衡。

sentences.en: For AMD, winning over Anthropic is a landmark victory—this company has been continuously investing in data center AI chips, and its MI series GPUs are considered one of the main competitors to NVIDIA's H series products; once Anthropic officially joins, AMD will not only enhance its presence among top AI laboratories, but also bring real incremental revenue for its data center AI chip business.
sentences.zh: 对于AMD来说，战胜Anthropic是一个里程碑式的胜利——这家公司一直在数据中心AI芯片上持续投入，其MI系列GPU被认为是NVIDIA H系列产品的主要竞争对手之一；一旦Anthropic正式加入，AMD不仅将增强其在顶级AI实验室中的地位，还将为其数据中心AI芯片业务带来真正的增量收入。

blocks.html_en: Anthropic's move reflects a systemic shift in the entire industry. As OpenAI, Google DeepMind, Meta AI, and other leading institutions continue to increase their demand for computing power, AI companies are collectively evaluating and expanding their chip supply sources, just to avoid the risk of putting all eggs in one basket. This collective turn objectively provides AMD, Intel, and other companies chasing NVIDIA, an opening to enter the core AI customer supply chain.

sentences.en: Anthropic's move reflects a systemic shift in the entire industry.
sentences.zh: Anthropic的举动反映了整个行业的系统性转变。

sentences.en: As OpenAI, Google DeepMind, Meta AI, and other leading institutions continue to increase their demand for computing power, AI companies are collectively evaluating and expanding their chip supply sources, just to avoid the risk of putting all eggs in one basket.
sentences.zh: 随着OpenAI、谷歌DeepMind、Meta AI等领先机构对算力的需求不断增加，AI企业正在集体评估和扩大芯片供应来源，只是为了避免把所有鸡蛋放在一个篮子里的风险。

sentences.en: This collective turn objectively provides AMD, Intel, and other companies chasing NVIDIA, an opening to enter the core AI customer supply chain.
sentences.zh: 这一集体转向客观上为AMD、英特尔等追逐NVIDIA的公司提供了进入核心AI客户供应链的机会。

content_html_zh:
一行不该暴露商业机密的公开代码，透露了Anthropic下一步的芯片布局。 据芯片行业分析公司SemiAnalysis称，AMD AI业务高级总监在GitHub上发布的代码明显留下了Anthropic作为AMD客户的痕迹。 这次非官方、非媒体的泄密事件发出了一个明确的信号：Anthropic 正在积极扩大其芯片来源，将其计算供应商从单一名单中解放出来。

周一，SemiAnalysis 在社交平台 X 上发布了分析，结论来自 AMD 高级总监对公共代码的专业剖析。 代码内容直接涉及Anthropic作为AMD客户的相关信息。 该机构表示将进一步解释代码细节和背景。 如果这一判断正确，AMD将在此前由NVIDIA和谷歌主导的高端AI训练市场获得新的地位，Anthropic的计算基础设施也将因此加速迈向多元化。

这个信息的流动路径比较特殊。 它不是通过官方公告或新闻报道，而是通过 AMD 内部高管推送到 GitHub 的代码。 SemiAnalysis 破译了这段代码，然后在 X 上公开分享了结论。 在科技行业，此类通过公共代码库意外泄露的案例并不少见——GitHub本来就可以搜索，而且发布者是AMD AI业务的高级总监，这使得信息的可信度相当高。 在帖子中，SemiAnalysis承诺将继续对代码逻辑进行层层分解，为市场提供更完整的解读。

事实上，在AMD这条线曝光之前，Anthropic的计算采购就已经远非单一路径了。 谷歌的TPU（张量处理单元）是其重要的计算源之一，这与谷歌对Anthropic的战略投资密切相关；此外，三星也被纳入其供应商体系。 现有的布局表明，Anthropic从未押注于单一点，而是刻意打造了多元化的供应商矩阵。 AMD的加入只会让这个网络在NVIDIA GPU之外又多了一个高性能计算选择。

对于 Anthropic 来说，将 AMD 纳入联盟具有多重战略意义。 随着模型训练和推理规模不断扩大，供应链风险、议价能力被压制以及被单一芯片供应商锁定特定技术路径带来的弊端日益显现。 通过同时拥抱谷歌TPU、三星和AMD，可以在成本控制、供应稳定性和技术灵活性之间重新找到更好的平衡。 对于AMD来说，战胜Anthropic是一个里程碑式的胜利——这家公司一直在数据中心AI芯片上持续投入，其MI系列GPU被认为是NVIDIA H系列产品的主要竞争对手之一；一旦Anthropic正式加入，AMD不仅将增强其在顶级AI实验室中的地位，还将为其数据中心AI芯片业务带来真正的增量收入。

Anthropic的举动反映了整个行业的系统性转变。 随着OpenAI、谷歌DeepMind、Meta AI等领先机构对算力的需求不断增加，AI企业正在集体评估和扩大芯片供应来源，只是为了避免把所有鸡蛋放在一个篮子里的风险。 这一集体转向客观上为AMD、英特尔等追逐NVIDIA的公司提供了进入核心AI客户供应链的机会。
